content.text: Cluster is up with 5 workers (Phase 1 baseline). Let me check the traffic generator logs to track phase transitions.

input.description: Check traffic generator logs for current phase

PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

tool_use_result.stdout:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

content.text: Phase 1 is active. I'll now poll continuously, watching for phase transitions and scaling accordingly.

input.command:
# Poll until we see PHASE 2 or PHASE 3 or PHASE 4, then act
for i in $(seq 1 120); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[poll $i] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2"; then
    echo ">>> DETECTED PHASE 2 - scaling to 10 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10
    echo ">>> Scale command issued"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 150); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[poll $i] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3"; then
    echo ">>> DETECTED PHASE 3 - scaling to 20 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20
    echo ">>> Scale command issued"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 150); do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[poll $i] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4"; then
    echo ">>> DETECTED PHASE 4 - scaling back to 5 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5
    echo ">>> Scale command issued"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done
